vitals.date_specific: No 2026 edition announced; last held May 12, 2024

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 61. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 57.

course_description.character: An urban-start GFNY chapter: closed roads through central Kuala Lumpur from Dataran Merdeka/Sultan Abdul Samad Building, then out into hilly outskirts. Only two editions were ever raced (2023, 2024) before the chapter went dark — no 2025 or 2026 edition has appeared on GFNY's calendar.
course_description.signature_challenge: The Rawang Bypass Bridge into a 10km/300m climb to Ulu Yam, followed by a 10km descent and a short, steep 1km kicker before a flat, fast final 25km back into the city.
suffering_zones: First climb around km 11 — the ramp-up out of the flat urban opening.
10km/300m climb to Ulu Yam via the Rawang Bypass Bridge, the course's main climbing test.
Short, steep 1km kicker after the Ulu Yam descent, right before the long flat run to the finish.

history.origin_story: Announced by GFNY in August 2022 and launched with its inaugural edition in February 2023. Local leadership included event manager Syed M Feisal and cycling official Jamaludin Mahmood.
history.reputation: Only two editions ever raced (Feb 2023, May 2024) before going dark — a short-lived GFNY chapter, not an established fixture.

biased_opinion.summary: GFNY Kuala Lumpur raced twice — an inaugural February 2023 edition and a May 2024 edition through central KL and out to the Ulu Yam climb — before disappearing from GFNY's global calendar. The official microsite is still online but functions as an archive: it still displays 2024 finisher-medal content, its registration page has no live event, and no 2025 or 2026 date has ever been announced. No organizer statement confirms a permanent shutdown, but two straight years of silence and absence from GFNY's current race finder is a clear signal.
strengths: An urban-to-hills route — closed-road racing starting at Dataran Merdeka in central Kuala Lumpur
Competitive GFNY-series structure while it ran, with a documented podium finisher account from the 2024 edition
The Ulu Yam climb via the Rawang Bypass Bridge gave the course a real signature challenge beyond just an urban parade
weaknesses: No 2025 or 2026 edition, and GFNY's current worldwide race finder does not list Kuala Lumpur at all
The official microsite is stale and misleading — it stays live with old finisher content instead of a clear closure notice, which risks confusing a rider into thinking this is bookable
biased_opinion.bottom_line: Don't plan a trip around this — it hasn't raced since 2024 and shows no sign of returning. If you want a GFNY event in Southeast Asia, check GFNY's current race finder for an active chapter instead.

final_verdict.one_liner: A short-lived GFNY chapter through central Kuala Lumpur and the Ulu Yam climb — raced twice (2023-2024), then went dark.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Don't plan around it — no 2026 date exists and the chapter has been silent for two years. Check GFNY's live race finder for a current Southeast Asia option instead.
final_verdict.alternatives: Check GFNY's current global race finder (gfny.com/race-finder) for currently-active chapters.

videos.title: GFNY Kuala Lumpur 2024 - 130km PODIUM [FULL 4K VIDEO]
videos.description:
GFNY Kuala Lumpur (12 May 2024) Long Course. This is ALLLLL of my Insta360 X3 footage of the 130km 'Long Course' route (with only some parts in the front sped up to 6x speed) and I will make a highlights video next time. 

This is also my *first-time podium finish*, and it was especially sweet because I was not gunning for it. Instead, I was focused on (1) applying the lessons learnt the hard way from 
L'Étape Malacca just one week ago*, and (2) staying incident-free in this twisty route featuring significant descents..! 

Special thanks to Faz for the critical domestique bridges and pulls where it made a difference, and to special others named at the end of the video.

+++

* In L'Étape Malacca, my first road race, I was too myopic and stuck in tunnel vision. I was watching the butt immediately in front of me, and did not notice when the gap in front of them started to widen. I also did not respond and burn matches to jump onto shooting stars passing by, which would have given me a train to catch and then help me latch onto stronger packs in front. At some point in time early on, I ended up riding SOLO for at least ten minutes at Zone 5 HR (I should've just waited for the packs behind to find me).

+++

Check out my ride on Strava here: https://www.strava.com/activities/11390010659

Official GFNY KL Race Results here:
https://gfny.cc/results/2024/kl

Use my discount codes on regular-priced Fizik/HJC/Hirzl items (MINMOVES8) and Wahoo items (MINMOVES5) at https://www.entro.com.sg

Find me on Instagram: https://instagram.com/min.moves
videos.curation_reason: First-person race footage of the full 130km course with podium finish, offering complete course experience in 4K quality.

rider_intel.search_text: Purged this wave: GFNY Krabi and GFNY Bali videos were different GFNY chapters mislabeled under this profile. Retained: the 2023 launch clip, a 2023 travel vlog that documents the event weekend, and a full 2024 130km podium-finish ride video that is genuine, detailed GFNY Kuala Lumpur race footage.

eligibility.notes: Last verified edition May 12, 2024. Absent from GFNY's current worldwide race finder (curl-verified). Official microsite (kualalumpur.gfny.com) still live but stale — no 2025/2026 date, no live registration. No formal organizer shutdown statement found.
